info.title: TW Analytics API
info.description:
BC Analytics API - Granular APIs for Marketing Dashboards.

    ## Features
    - Full dashboard APIs with all metrics and charts
    - Individual metric endpoints for Knowledge Graph
    - Individual chart endpoints with data point access
    - Shift-click context capture support
    - Date range and granularity filters on all endpoints

Readiness probe — are all dependencies reachable?
Get Snowflake connection pool status.
API root - returns basic info.
Get current feature flag status for all dashboards.

post.description:
Clear cached data, scoped to the caller's authority.

super_admin clears every tenant's cache; any lower admin (tenant_admin)
clears ONLY their own tenant's keys. Previously this unconditionally ran
``clear_pattern("*")``, so a single-tenant tenant_admin could evict every
tenant's cached dashboard/metric data platform-wide (cross-tenant cache
stampede + warehouse-cost amplification — audit finding L1). Cache keys
are tenant-prefixed ``<slug>:<prefix>:<hash>`` (tw_shared.cache.manager),
so scoping the glob by the caller's slug confines the flush.
Create a new user account. Disabled by default in v2 — set SIGNUP_ENABLED=true to allow. Invite flow is the only onboarding path in production.

get.description:
List tenants accessible to the caller per Q1 strict-isolation.

Per S13d.2 hotfix (F4): super_admin → all tenants; agency_admin →
own agency + child tenants; tenant_admin / analyst / viewer →
bound tenant only. The result populates the tenant switcher
dropdown (and any other admin listing) without leaking tenants
outside the caller's accessible set. Switching tenants is done
via the ``tw_active_tenant`` cookie, not by widening this listing.

Note: in BC, this route is currently shadowed by
``app/features/admin/router.py`` (which requires super_admin only)
for ``GET /api/v1/admin/tenants``. The caller-aware filter here
defends DC + CIQ adoption (60-day window) where the BC shadow
does not apply.
List all dashboards available for the current tenant context.

Dashboard usage analytics. Admin only.

Get tenant details for a slug the caller has access to.

Per S13d.2 hotfix (F4 follow-on, caught during live verification):
pre-hotfix this route returned tenant details to any caller with
``TENANT_READ`` regardless of whether the requested tenant was in
the caller's accessible set — e.g., a ``growthco`` agency_admin
could fetch ``rare_seeds`` details. Out-of-scope slugs now answer
404 to avoid leaking tenant existence.

post.description: Soft-delete a tenant.

Provision a new tenant (PG schema + Snowflake DB).

Per S15 + S13d.2 hotfix (F5(a) cache invariant): the row is
created with its Snowflake credential triple populated by
convention from the tenant slug:

* Per-tenant (always): ``USR_<UPPER_SLUG>`` / ``RL_<UPPER_SLUG>``
  / ``WH_<UPPER_SLUG>`` on ``snowflake_user/role/warehouse``.
* Agency (only when ``body.tenant_type == 'agency'``): also sets
  ``USR_<UPPER_SLUG>_AGENCY`` / ``RL_<UPPER_SLUG>_AGENCY`` /
  ``WH_<UPPER_SLUG>_AGENCY`` on the ``agency_snowflake_*``
  columns. The matching SF objects (user / role / warehouse with
  cross-DB grants) are provisioned out-of-band via
  ``docs/operations/snowflake_role_isolation_ddl.sql`` — Akshay
  runs this on the SF account; this endpoint only stamps the
  identifiers on the PG row.

Pre-S15 the row landed with NULL ``snowflake_user/role`` — that
was finding F5(a). Cache poison is now blocked by the registry
invariant in S13d.2 even when SF DDL hasn't run yet.

get.description:
Return the tenants the current user can access.

Uses the RBAC ``get_current_user`` dependency for authentication.
Falls back to cookie-based JWT parsing for user UUID resolution
(needed for the PG query against ``user_tenants``).

Quarterly access review for the caller's bound tenant.

Per S13d.2 hotfix (F3): filtered by the caller's ``tenant_id`` at
the query level. Super_admin and agency_admin switch tenants via
the ``tw_active_tenant`` cookie to audit users in other tenants.

Returns an empty user list if the caller has no bound tenant
(crash-over-leak — no default fallback). The aggregate keys
(``total_users``, ``inactive_90d``) are still present so the
consumer JSON shape stays stable.

post.description:
Purge audit_log entries older than *retention_days*.

SOC 2 default is 2555 days (~7 years). Only SUPER_ADMIN may
invoke; relies on the S4 ``super_admin_bypass`` RLS policy
(alembic 023) to scope the DELETE across all tenants rather
than just the operator's current tenant.

Delete expired token revocation records.

Keeps the revoked_tokens table from growing unboundedly. The
inner :func:`purge_expired_tokens` resolves the platform engine
itself (system-scoped, since ``revoked_tokens`` has no
``tenant_id``); the request-scoped session is pulled here purely
as defence-in-depth so future audit-log additions inherit RLS
context.

get.description:
Return current TLS and security configuration status.

Useful for SOC 2 auditors to verify that production is correctly
hardened (secure cookies, restricted CORS, HTTPS enforcement).

Paginated audit log for the caller's bound tenant.

The PG row-level security policy on platform.audit_log
(tenant_isolation + super_admin_bypass) bounds the rows to the
caller's active tenant. We additionally explicit-filter by
tenant_id for clarity in the SQL.
Single audit_log row by id — RLS-scoped to caller's tenant.

Stream CSV of audit_log rows matching the filters (no pagination).

Caps at 10k rows to avoid blowing memory on the api worker — for
larger exports, use the archive pipeline (Phase E1.5).

Cross-tenant billing + usage snapshot.

Pulls three things in one round trip:
  1. ``SHOW WAREHOUSES`` + ``SHOW RESOURCE MONITORS`` for the
     per-warehouse quota dashboard.
  2. ``QUERY_HISTORY`` grouped by (user, role, warehouse, db)
     to attribute work + cost.
  3. The top 20 expensive queries by elapsed time in the window
     (debug hook for "why is this tenant burning credits").
Cross-tenant audit_log list. super_admin only.

get.description:
Walk every tenant's audit_log SHA-256 chain and report integrity.

super_admin only. Mirrors the
:mod:`backend.scripts.verify_audit_chain` CLI by calling the
canonical in-process implementation at
``packages/shared/tw_shared/audit/chain_verifier.py::_verify_once``
(alembic 026 + 028 land the chain columns + the writer that
populates them; verifier walks ``platform.audit_log`` in
``(created_at, id)`` order and recomputes each row's
``payload_hash`` from ``prev_hash`` + canonical row contents).

The library issues ``SET app.role='super_admin'`` itself
(``chain_verifier.py:86``) so the ``super_admin_bypass`` RLS
policy lets it read across tenants. Runs synchronously off-thread
via :func:`asyncio.to_thread` so a busy chain doesn't block the
event loop.

Returns ``chain_intact=True`` when every tenant's chain
recomputes; ``False`` plus per-tenant mismatch row IDs when any
row was tampered with after insert.
Fetch a single row by id, regardless of tenant. super_admin only.
Stream CSV of cross-tenant audit_log rows. Caps at 10k rows.

post.description:
Drop every cached Snowflake engine.

Used by the rotation CLI after ``ALTER USER … SET PASSWORD`` so the
next request reconnects with the rotated credential. Existing
in-flight requests using the old engine continue until their
connections close — they don't see the new password until the next
pool checkout.

Drop one or every cached PostgreSQL engine.

Used by ``rotate_pg_password.py`` after ``ALTER ROLE … SET PASSWORD``
so the next request reconnects with the rotated credential.
In-flight requests using the old engine continue until their
connections close — they don't see the new password until the next
pool checkout.

Returns all metrics and charts for the CEO Pulse dashboard.

- ?platform=google → Only Google Ads metrics
- ?platform=meta   → Only Meta Ads metrics

For migration validation, use `use_mart` parameter:
- ?use_mart=true  → Forces mart repository (new logic)
- ?use_mart=false → Forces legacy repository (old logic)
- (omit)          → Uses feature flag setting

get.description:
Get metadata about the Weekly Exec dashboard.

Returns the latest available date which should be used as the default
for date range selection.

Get full Weekly Executive Summary dashboard data.

Returns all metrics and charts for the Weekly Executive Summary dashboard.
Use this endpoint for initial page load.

**Filters:**
- **date_from/date_to**: Date range for the current period
- **compare_from/compare_to**: Optional custom comparison period
- **granularity**: Time granularity (daily, weekly, monthly)
- **platform**: Filter by ad platform (google, meta, or None for all)
- **channel_type**: Filter by channel type (DISPLAY, SEARCH, SHOPPING, VIDEO, PERFORMANCE_MAX)
- **comparison_mode**: Comparison mode (wow, mom, yoy, custom)

**Metrics included:**
- Revenue (with WoW/MoM/YoY comparisons)
- Spend
- ROAS
- Orders
- Sessions
- Conversion Rate

**Data sections:**
- KPI Summary with sparklines
- Channel Performance Matrix (2x2 quadrant)
- Top 5 Wins
- Top 5 Concerns
- Budget Pacing by Channel
- Forecast vs Actual
- 8-Week Trend
- Channel Performance Table
- Decision Recommendations

get.description:
Get full Quarterly Review dashboard data.

Returns all KPIs, charts, and analysis data for the Quarterly Strategic Review:

- **KPIs**: Revenue, Ad Spend, ROAS, New Customers with YoY comparison
- **Channel Growth**: Year-over-year channel performance
- **Seasonality**: Monthly seasonality patterns by channel
- **Customer Acquisition**: Customer growth trends (Magento-based)
- **Channel Saturation**: ROAS efficiency over cumulative spend
- **Strategic Initiatives**: PMAX, Video, Shopping, Search campaigns
- **Market Opportunities**: Growth potential by channel
- **Efficiency Metrics**: CPC, CPA, CPM trends by platform

Data Sources (Mart Mode - Default):
- MART_CEO_DAILY_PULSE: KPIs (revenue, spend, ROAS)
- MART_ATTRIBUTION_DAILY: Channel-attributed revenue
- MART_MARKETING_DAILY: Efficiency metrics
- MART_GOOGLE_CAMPAIGN_DAILY: Campaign performance

Use this endpoint for initial page load.

get.description:
Get all root nodes (metrics with no causes).

Used as the starting point for Focus Mode.
These are the "first causes" in the causal chain.

Get all leaf nodes (metrics with no effects).

These are terminal metrics - the final effects in causal chains.

Get KPI hierarchy organized by category.

Used for Focus Mode starting point - shows which KPIs exist
and how they're categorized.

Get direct children of a metric.

Used for Focus Mode expansion - when user clicks "+" button.
Returns only immediate effects, not full descendants.

Get direct parents of a metric.

Used for understanding immediate causes.

Get all upstream causes (ancestors) of a metric.

**Use for root cause analysis**: "What's driving this metric down?"

Returns all metrics that directly or indirectly cause this one.

Example: get_upstream("revenue")
-> Returns: [traffic, conversion_rate, aov, sessions, clicks, ...]

Get all downstream effects (descendants) of a metric.

**Use for impact analysis**: "What will break if this metric fails?"

Returns all metrics that are directly or indirectly affected by this one.

Example: get_downstream("cpc")
-> Returns: [cpa, roas, revenue, profit_margin, ...]

Find the shortest causal path between two metrics.

**Use for explanation**: "How does CPC affect Revenue?"

Returns ordered list of metrics in the causal chain.

Example: find_path(from="cpc", to="revenue")
-> Returns: [cpc, cpa, conversion_value, revenue]

Calculate the blast radius if a metric degrades.

**Use for severity assessment**: "How bad is this breach?"

Returns:
- direct_effects: Immediate children
- total_affected: Count of all downstream metrics
- affected_kpis: Which KPIs will be impacted
- severity_score: 0-100 based on KPI impact ratio

Example: blast_radius("cpc")
-> Shows that increasing CPC affects ROAS, CAC, and ultimately Revenue

Get the local neighborhood of a metric.

Returns both parents and children within the specified depth.
Useful for visualizing the immediate context of a metric.

get.description:
Get metadata about the Product Overview dashboard.

Returns the latest available date which should be used as the default
for date filtering. Also provides data freshness information.

Get full Product Overview dashboard data.

Returns all metrics and charts for the Product Overview dashboard.
Use this endpoint for initial page load.

Metrics include:
- revenue: Total product revenue
- units: Total units sold
- avg_price: Average price per item
- conversion: Purchase-to-view conversion rate

Charts include:
- revenue_trend: Revenue trend over time
- funnel: View-to-purchase funnel
- top_products: Top products by revenue
- categories: Category performance treemap
- product_matrix: Revenue vs growth matrix
- seasonal: Seasonal performance heatmap
- new_products: Recently launched products
- conversion_analysis: Top converting products
- opportunities: Product optimization opportunities

Get all metrics for the Product Overview dashboard.

Returns all 4 metrics in a single response.

schema.title: Response List Metrics Api V1 Product Overview Metrics  Get

get.description:
Get Revenue metric.

Returns total product revenue.

Get Units Sold metric.

Returns total units purchased.

Get Average Price metric.

Returns average price per product.

Get Conversion Rate metric.

Returns purchase-to-view conversion rate.

Get all charts for the Product Overview dashboard.

Returns all 9 charts in a single response.

schema.title: Response List Charts Api V1 Product Overview Charts  Get

get.description:
Get Revenue Trend chart.

Returns revenue trend over time as an area chart.
Supports daily, weekly, or monthly granularity.

Get Product View to Purchase Funnel chart.

Returns funnel data: Views -> Cart Adds -> Purchases.
Includes conversion rates between stages.

Get Top Products chart.

Returns top 50 products by revenue as a table.
Includes units, avg price, views, cart rate, conversion rate.

Get Category Performance chart.

Returns category hierarchy treemap data.
Includes L1, L2, L3 category breakdown by revenue.

Get Product Performance Matrix chart.

Returns scatter plot data: Revenue (x) vs Growth % (y).
Products are classified into quadrants: stars, rising-stars, cash-cows, dogs.

Get Seasonal Performance chart.

Returns heatmap data: Year x Month matrix with revenue values.
Useful for identifying seasonal patterns.

Get New Products Tracking chart.

Returns recently launched products (last 60 days).
Includes launch date, days live, revenue, conversion rate.

Get Conversion Rate Analysis chart.

Returns top converting products (500+ views).
Sorted by purchase-to-view conversion rate.

Get Product Opportunities chart.

Returns products with optimization potential:
- Optimize: High views, low conversion
- Promote: High conversion, low views

Get metadata about the Product Category dashboard.

Returns the latest available date which should be used as the default
for the dashboard view.

Get full Product Category dashboard data.

Returns all metrics and charts for the Product Category dashboard.
Use this endpoint for initial page load.

Category level options:
- 2: Top level (e.g., "Seeds")
- 3: Second level (e.g., "Vegetable Seeds")
- 4: Third level (e.g., "Tomato Seeds")
- 5: Most granular (e.g., "Heirloom Tomato Seeds")

  - Multiple values: `?order_status=complete,processing`

- **customer_type**: Filter by customer type
  - `new`: First-time customers
  - `returning`: Repeat customers
  - `guest`: Guest checkout (no account)

- **store_view**: Filter by Magento store view ID
  - Single value: `?store_view=1`
  - Multiple values: `?store_view=1,2,3`

## Metrics included
- revenue, orders, aov, customers, completion_rate, conversion_rate
- new_customer_orders, returning_customer_orders

## Charts included
- revenue_trend, orders_trend, customer_mix, top_products
- status_distribution, orders_heatmap

UserRole.title: UserRole
UserRole.description:
Persisted user role — superset of :class:`Role` for legacy back-compat.

Pre-EP-04 ``platform.users.role`` rows used ``"admin"`` / ``"user"``;
EP-04's migration introduced the canonical names. :class:`Role`
represents the 5 canonical values used by RBAC (and seeded by
alembic 018); :class:`UserRole` is what may appear in the database
row including the 2 legacy aliases. Validation downstream maps
legacy values through :data:`tw_shared.auth.rbac.LEGACY_ROLE_MAP`.

Access hierarchy (see ``backend/app/features/auth/access.py``):

- ``SUPER_ADMIN``  : platform owner, can access any tenant
- ``AGENCY_ADMIN`` : manages an agency tenant + cascades to all
                     agency_client children
- ``TENANT_ADMIN`` : manages one standalone tenant OR one
                     agency_client
- ``ANALYST``      : read + dashboard access on one tenant
- ``VIEWER``       : read-only on one tenant
- ``ADMIN`` / ``USER`` : legacy aliases kept for pre-migration rows
